calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-28-2009 Off Calendar

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Sep-17-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Lois Jones, #3689.

Plaintiff Daniel L. Balsam, Esq. was present in pro per, placed under oath examined.
No appearance by defendant Amanda Greiner, an individual.
Default was entered against defendant on May 22, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed on July 10, 2009.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Daniel L. Balsam, and against the Defaulted Defendant Amanda Greiner, an individual as follows:

Damages/Principal - $19,000.00.
Attorney's Fees - $5,574.06.
Costs - $585.00, for a total judgmen in the amount of $25,159.06.

Additionally, plaintiff's request for punitive damages is DENIED. Lastly, any violations of this injunction shall subject defendant Amanda Greiner, an individual, to damages in the amount of $2,500.00 per violation, pursuant to B&P Section 17206.

Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Off Calendar Sep-01-2009 Continued To Sep-17-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 218
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing.
Dept. 218, Comm. William R. Gargano, Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, CSR: Maria Lertora, CSR #11935.

Timothy J. Walton, Esq. appeared with plaintiff Daniel L. Balsam, who was placed under oath but did not testify.
No appearance by defendant Amanda Greiner, an individual.
Default was entered against defendant on May 22, 2009.
Doe defendants were dismissed on July 10, 2009.

The Court requires further briefing on the Federal preemption issue in order to render a decision. Good cause appearing, this matter is continued to September 17, 2009 @ 9:00 a.m. in Department 218.
